$comment: Elevation boundary matrix — classifies every user-facing action by admin requirement. Non-mutating workflows run without elevation; mutating workflows prompt on execution.

executionLevel.notes: The PowerShell script and PS2EXE exe self-elevate immediately. The WPF shell launches as invoker and prompts for elevation only when executing mutating actions.

actions.description: Compares pinned dependency versions against upstream; reads only
actions.notes: Only backend action that skips the admin requirement check in MainViewModel

actions.description: Downloads and applies SpotX + Spicetify customizations to Spotify
actions.notes: Primary install workflow. Requires admin to write to Spotify's AppData files

actions.description: Refreshes SpotX patches and restores saved Spicetify state
Reinstalls Marketplace custom app, applies, and opens marketplace
Opens spotify:app:marketplace when Marketplace is already installed
actions.notes: Open-only action; repair remains the mutating path

actions.description: Runs spicetify restore to disable customizations — reversible via Reapply

actions.notes: Creates a Spicetify config backup without modifying Spotify
Restores Spicetify backup and reapplies — modifies Spotify files

actions.description: Removes Spicetify customizations while keeping SpotX in place
Headless watcher that reapplies patches after Spotify updates
actions.notes: Runs as a scheduled task at logon; elevation inherited from task registration

actions.description: Restores Spotify, removes Spicetify CLI, config, and PATH entry
8-phase comprehensive uninstaller: process kill, AppX, filesystem, registry, tasks, firewall, verification
Unregisters the watcher scheduled task and removes LibreSpot config, backups, logs, and crash reports. Does not affect Spotify or Spicetify.
actions.notes: Marked IsDestructive in AppCatalog. Does not require admin because it only removes per-user LibreSpot data.

actions.description: Generates a structured preflight plan for the current config without mutating disk, PATH, tasks, or registry. Emits plan entries via the event protocol.
actions.notes: Read-only dry-run surface. Does not require admin. Fleet CLI exposes this as --dry-run.

nonActionWorkflows.notes: Config UI is available without elevation in the WPF shell
Windows app notifications are not supported for elevated processes
Future. URI activation should land in the non-elevated shell and prompt for elevation if the action requires it

designPrinciples: The WPF shell launches non-elevated (asInvoker) and prompts for admin only when executing mutating backend actions
Non-mutating workflows (config browse, update check, diagnostics, logs, notifications) never require elevation
Destructive actions (FullReset, UninstallSpicetify, RemoveSelfData) are gated behind both elevation and confirmation
Toast/notification support is only available in the non-elevated session — elevated processes cannot register for app notifications
The PowerShell script self-elevates immediately because its architecture does not separate read-only and mutating paths
